summary:
The true statements concerning direct ophthalmoscopy include that images appear immediate but may be perceived by observers differently from standard projections; having an approximate two-disk diameter viewing area provides adequate detail for most examinations regardless slight differences among equipment types; self-luminescence ensures functionality under various conditions including low-light settings while specific claims around standardized magnifying factors require qualification due variability between instruments used clinically today.
The false statement must therefore pertain specifically towards any assertion made solely based off rigid numerical specifications like fixed 'five-times' magnification not universally applicable given existing technological diversity within diagnostic tools employed presently throughout medicine practice globally.
To summarize concisely per instruction provided earlier focusing only essential aspects pertinent answering original query posed initially : 
Key Clinical Points Regarding Direct Ophthalmoscopy Include Immediate Visual Image But May Appear Inverted Depending On Observer Experience And Typically Offers About Two-Disc Diameter Field Of Vision With Variable Magnification Capabilities Ranging From Five To Ten Times Across Different Models Used Clinically Today Self-Illumination Ensures Functionality Under Various Lighting Conditions Especially During Nighttime Exams Without External Light Sources Available . False Statement Must Be Specific Numerical Specification Such As Fixed ‘Five-Times’ Magnification Not Universally Applicable Due Technological Diversity Within Diagnostic Tools Employed Currently Globally .
 Agent2 summarized the critical information related to each option presented in relation to whether those were accurate representations of how direct ophthalmoscopy functions according to available medical documentation. Specifically addressing what makes one choice incorrect compared against other verifiable facts highlighted previously such as variable vs precise measurements , real-time observation versus delayed imaging processes etc... This allowed pinpointing exactly why certain details couldnt hold up when evaluated alongside broader context established through research review process conducted here before final conclusion drawn pointing out non-uniformity surrounding stated level of magnification capability claimed exclusively thus marking it as least likely correct answer offered originally .

In summary,
1) Images seen instantly though possibly appearing upside down
2) Viewable region roughly matches size spanned by couple retinal discs widely enough yet varies minimally instrument-to-instrument
3) Actual zoom levels differ significantly ranging generally anywhere above minimum threshold close vicinity mentioned typically falling somewhere middle range specified instead i.e., ~x5-x10X
